summary:
Claim: Cholesterol is the most abundant component in HDL compared to other lipoproteins.  
Evidence: - Cholesteryl esters account for ~70% of HDL’s core content, significantly more than free cholesterol or triglycerides. - Triglyceride levels are minimal (<1%) in HDL, dropping sharply from VLDL/IDL.  
Answer: A
